Title: Check if EBS Default Encryption is activated.
Description: EBS Default Encryption is not activated.

RelatedRequirements: NIST-800-53-Revision-5 au_9_3 cm_6_a cm_9_b cp_9_d sc_8_3 sc_8_

Recommendation.Text: Enable Encryption. Use a CMK where possible. It will provide additional management and privacy benefits.

Title: Ensure there are no EBS Volumes unencrypted.
Description: EBS Snapshot vol-07a5b11712caef09d is unencrypted.

RelatedRequirements: NIST-800-53-Revision-5 au_9_3 cm_6_a cm_9_b cp_9_d sc_8_3 sc_8_

Recommendation.Text: Encrypt all EBS volumes and Enable Encryption by default You can configure your AWS account to enforce the encryption of the new EBS volumes and snapshot copies that you create. For example; Amazon EBS encrypts the EBS volumes created when you launch an instance and the snapshots that you copy from an unencrypted snapshot.

Title: Check if EBS snapshots exists.
Description: Snapshots not found for the EBS volume vol-07a5b11712caef09d.

Recommendation.Text: Creating point-in-time EBS snapshots periodically will allow you to handle efficiently your data recovery process in the event of a failure, to save your data before shutting down an EC2 instance, to back up data for geographical expansion and to maintain your disaster recovery stack up to date.

Title: Check if EC2 instances have detailed monitoring enabled.
Description: EC2 Instance i-050f86e1c60908fda does not have detailed monitoring enabled.

Recommendation.Text: Enable detailed monitoring for EC2 instances to gain better insights into performance metrics.

Title: Check if S3 buckets have ACLs enabled
Description: S3 Bucket vulnerable-lab-bucket-161eb717 has bucket ACLs disabled.

Recommendation.Text: Ensure that S3 ACLs are disabled (BucketOwnerEnforced). Use IAM policies and bucket policies to manage access.

Title: Check if S3 buckets have default encryption (SSE) enabled or use a bucket policy to enforce it.
Description: S3 Bucket vulnerable-lab-bucket-161eb717 has Server Side Encryption with AES256.

RelatedRequirements: NIST-800-53-Revision-5 au_9_3 cm_6_a cm_9_b cp_9_d cp_9_8 pm_11

Recommendation.Text: Ensure that S3 buckets have encryption at rest enabled.

Title: Check if S3 buckets have KMS encryption enabled.
Description: Server Side Encryption is not configured with kms for S3 Bucket vulnerable-lab-bucket-161eb717.

Recommendation.Text: Ensure that S3 buckets have encryption at rest enabled using KMS.

Title: Check S3 Bucket Level Public Access Block.
Description: Block Public Access is not configured for the S3 Bucket vulnerable-lab-bucket-161eb717.

Recommendation.Text: You can enable Public Access Block at the bucket level to prevent the exposure of your data stored in S3.

Title: Check if S3 bucket MFA Delete is not enabled.
Description: S3 Bucket vulnerable-lab-bucket-161eb717 has MFA Delete disabled.

Recommendation.Text: Adding MFA delete to an S3 bucket, requires additional authentication when you change the version state of your bucket or you delete and object version adding another layer of security in the event your security credentials are compromised or unauthorized access is granted.

Title: Check if S3 buckets have object lock enabled
Description: S3 Bucket vulnerable-lab-bucket-161eb717 has Object Lock disabled.

Recommendation.Text: Ensure that your Amazon S3 buckets have Object Lock feature enabled in order to prevent the objects they store from being deleted.

Title: Check if S3 buckets have object versioning enabled
Description: S3 Bucket vulnerable-lab-bucket-161eb717 has versioning disabled.

RelatedRequirements: FedRamp-Moderate-Revision-4 au-9-2 cp-9-b cp-10 sc-5 si-12
NIST-800-53-Revision-5 au_9_2 cp_1_2 cp_2_5 cp_6_a cp_6_1 cp_6_
NIST-CSF-1.1 be_5 ds_4 ip_4 ip_9 pt_5 rp_1 rp_1

Recommendation.Text: Configure versioning using the Amazon console or API for buckets with sensitive information that is changing frequently; and backup may not be enough to capture all the changes.

Title: Check if S3 buckets have policies which allow WRITE access.
Description: S3 Bucket vulnerable-lab-bucket-161eb717 does not have a bucket policy.

RelatedRequirements: FedRamp-Moderate-Revision-4 ac-3 ac-4 ac-6 ac-17-1 ac-21-b cm-2
NIST-800-53-Revision-5 ac_2_6 ac_3 ac_3_7 ac_4_21 ac_6 ac_17_b
NIST-800-53-Revision-4 ac_3 ac_4 ac_6 ac_21 sc_7_3 sc_7
NIST-CSF-1.1 ac_3 ac_5 ds_5 ip_8 pt_3

Recommendation.Text: Ensure proper bucket policy is in place with the least privilege principle applied.

Title: Ensure there are no S3 buckets open to Everyone or Any AWS user.
Description: S3 Bucket vulnerable-lab-bucket-161eb717 is not public.

RelatedRequirements: FedRamp-Moderate-Revision-4 ac-3 ac-4 ac-6 ac-17-1 ac-21-b cm-2
NIST-800-53-Revision-5 ac_2_6 ac_3 ac_3_7 ac_4_21 ac_6 ac_17_b
NIST-800-53-Revision-4 ac_3 ac_4 ac_6 ac_21 sc_7_3 sc_7
NIST-CSF-1.1 ac_3 ac_5 ds_5 ip_8 pt_3

Recommendation.Text: You can enable block public access settings only for access points, buckets and AWS accounts. Amazon S3 does not support block public access settings on a per-object basis. When you apply block public access settings to an account; the settings apply to all AWS Regions globally. The settings might not take effect in all Regions immediately or simultaneously, but they eventually propagate to all Regions.

Title: Ensure there are no S3 buckets listable by Everyone or Any AWS customer.
Description: S3 Bucket vulnerable-lab-bucket-161eb717 is not publicly listable.

Recommendation.Text: You can enable block public access settings only for access points, buckets and AWS accounts. Amazon S3 does not support block public access settings on a per-object basis. When you apply block public access settings to an account; the settings apply to all AWS Regions globally. The settings might not take effect in all Regions immediately or simultaneously, but they eventually propagate to all Regions.

Title: Ensure there are no S3 buckets writable by Everyone or Any AWS customer.
Description: S3 Bucket vulnerable-lab-bucket-161eb717 is not publicly writable.

Recommendation.Text: You can enable block public access settings only for access points, buckets and AWS accounts. Amazon S3 does not support block public access settings on a per-object basis. When you apply block public access settings to an account; the settings apply to all AWS Regions globally. The settings might not take effect in all Regions immediately or simultaneously, but they eventually propagate to all Regions.

Title: Check if S3 buckets have secure transport policy.
Description: S3 Bucket vulnerable-lab-bucket-161eb717 does not have a bucket policy, thus it allows HTTP requests.

RelatedRequirements: FedRamp-Moderate-Revision-4 ac-17-2 sc-7 sc-8-1 sc-8 sc-23
NIST-800-53-Revision-5 ac_4 ac_4_22 ac_17_2 ac_24_1 au_9_3 ca_9

Recommendation.Text: Ensure that S3 buckets have encryption in transit enabled.

Title: Check if S3 buckets have server access logging enabled
Description: S3 Bucket vulnerable-lab-bucket-161eb717 has server access logging disabled.

NIST-CSF-1.1 ae_1 ae_3 ae_4 cm_1 cm_3 cm_6 cm_7 am_3 ac_6 ds_5

Recommendation.Text: Ensure that S3 buckets have Logging enabled. CloudTrail data events can be used in place of S3 bucket logging. If that is the case, this finding can be considered a false positive.
